text:
**TL;DR.** Yes. The route I would bet on is **asymptotic strong Feller + topological irreducibility** (HairerвҖ“Mattingly 2006). It is purpose-built to *bypass* pointwise transition AC. Markov uniqueness is clean but does not by itself exclude О· вҠҘ ПҖ. The finite-dim stationary-FP / Fisher-info route is the most direct attack on О· вүӘ ПҖ but is technically the heaviest because of the distributional drift.

**(i) Asymptotic strong Feller (ASF) + topological irreducibility.** HM06 Thm. 3.16 (and HM11 Thm. 3.4): if P_t is ASF at every x and topologically irreducible, the invariant probability is unique. ASF is a quantitative gradient bound that *can* blow up as tвҶ’0, so it's genuinely weaker than strong Feller. The input you need:

вҖ” A uniform-in-n BismutвҖ“ElworthyвҖ“Li gradient bound for the regularized P_t^n,
|D P_t^n f(x)[h]|ВІ вүӨ C(t) ||f||_вҲһВІ ||h||_EВІ,
with C(t) depending only on the *bounded potential* (0 вүӨ B_n вүӨ 1, B_n' вүҘ 0 monotone ramp), **not** on ||G_{1/n}||_вҲһ or ||G_{1/n}||_2. This is the technical heart.
вҖ” It's enough to obtain the weaker "approximate gradient bound" |вҲҮP_t f|_E вүӨ Оө||f||_вҲһ + Оҙ(t)||вҲҮf||_E (with Оҙ(t)вҶ’0 quickly enough), uniformly in n; pass to the limit.

Topological irreducibility should be cheap: approximate controllability of вҲӮ_t v = ВҪО”_D v + bounded forcing on [0,1] is classical, and the noise has full CameronвҖ“Martin support.

**(ii) Asymptotic coupling** (HM "Yet another look at Harris", Glatt-HoltzвҖ“MattinglyвҖ“Richards). Equivalent in spirit but constructive. Couple (u^x, Е©^y) using a Girsanov shift g so О”_t = u^x_t вҲ’ Е©^y_t вҶ’ 0 in C_0. Specific lemmas:
(L1) Continuous dependence x вҶҰ u^x in C([0,T], C_0) in probability (from ABLM 2024 stability вҖ” verify their estimates are uniform on bounded initial data).
(L2) Novikov-type bound рқ”ј exp(ВҪвҲ«вӮҖ^T ||g_s||ВІ ds) вүӨ C on compacts.
(L3) О”_t вҶ’ 0 in probability under the shifted measure.
The reason this should work: gradient of *bounded* potential вҮ’ controllable Girsanov entropy; Dirichlet spectral gap ПҖВІ gives linear contraction in the absence of drift mismatch.

**(iii) Markov uniqueness** (Eberle 1999 Lect. Notes; AlbeverioвҖ“RГ¶ckner; Stannat 1999 Mem. AMS). The BZ form on smooth cylinders FC_b^вҲһ should be **essentially m-dissipative** вҮ’ unique Markovian LВІ(ПҖ) extension вҮ’ ABLM and BZ generate the same LВІ(ПҖ) semigroup. But this still gives only "ПҖ-AC invariant measure is unique" вҖ” exactly the round-1 conclusion. To eliminate О· вҠҘ ПҖ you need (i), (ii), or (iv).

**(iv) Finite-dim FP + Fisher info** (BogachevвҖ“KrylovвҖ“RГ¶cknerвҖ“Shaposhnikov "FPK Equations" AMS 2015; Da PratoвҖ“RГ¶cknerвҖ“Stannat 2003). For any invariant О·, project О·_N onto first N DirichletвҖ“Fourier modes; aim for I(О·_N | ПҖ_N) вүӨ C uniformly in N, then О· вүӘ ПҖ in the projective limit. Obstruction: the projected stationary FP equation
вҲ« L_N^* F dО·_N = 0 for cylinder F
contains Оҙ_0(u(x_*)) tested against derivatives of F; you need a *robust* interpretation of this against an arbitrary invariant О·. The bounded-potential gradient structure is in your favor (write Оҙ_0 = ВҪ(2H_+)' and integrate by parts at the level of О· in cylinder coordinates), but I am uncertain how clean this becomes.

**(v) Weak Feller + LВІ gap + topological irreducibility is NOT enough.** Classical Doob needs strong Feller (or a small set / minorization). The standard replacement *is* (i).

**Concrete recommendation.** Push hard on (i). The bounded-potential structure is precisely the mechanism that made your uniform PoincarГ© (5) survive the limit; it should *also* make a Bismut-type gradient bound survive the limit because the relevant quantity is the *energy* of B_n, not the sup-norm of G_{1/n}. Useful template: HM 2018 "Strong Feller for singular SPDEs" вҖ” their parabolic-Anderson-style argument is the closest analogue. Also worth a look: KomorowskiвҖ“PeszatвҖ“Szarek (Ann. Probab. 2010) **e-property** framework, often easier to verify than ASF.
